'Commonwealth of Puerto Rico' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (agency). General-obligation (GO) debt issuer and central government.
'Financial Oversight and Management Board for Puerto Rico' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (agency). PROMESA Title III fiscal oversight board.
'Puerto Rico Fiscal Agency and Financial Advisory Authority' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (agency). Fiscal agent and financial advisor to the government.
'Puerto Rico Public-Private Partnerships Authority' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (agency). Authority that structures public-private partnership transactions.
'Puerto Rico Electric Power Authority' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (utility). Public electric power utility and debt issuer.
'Puerto Rico Aqueduct and Sewer Authority' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (utility). Public water and sewer utility and debt issuer.
'Puerto Rico Highways and Transportation Authority' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (agency). Highways and transportation authority and debt issuer.
'Puerto Rico Sales Tax Financing Corporation' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (fund). Sales-tax-backed bond issuing corporation.
'Government Development Bank for Puerto Rico' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (agency). Former fiscal agent and development bank.
'Puerto Rico Department of Economic Development and Commerce' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (agency). Economic development and commerce department.
'Puerto Rico Public Buildings Authority' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (agency). Public buildings authority and debt issuer.
'LUMA Energy' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (firm). Private transmission and distribution operator under P3 concession.
'Genera PR' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (firm). Private power-generation operator under P3 concession.
'Autopistas Metropolitanas de Puerto Rico' is a Puerto Rico public-money institution (firm). Toll-road concessionaire on long-term HTA agreements.
The central government of Puerto Rico has its administrative seat in San Juan.
AAFAF is headquartered in San Juan Puerto Rico.
The Puerto Rico P3 Authority is headquartered in San Juan Puerto Rico.
PREPA has its headquarters in San Juan Puerto Rico.
PRASA has its headquarters in San Juan Puerto Rico.
The Highways and Transportation Authority is headquartered in San Juan Puerto Rico.
COFINA is domiciled in San Juan Puerto Rico.
The Government Development Bank is headquartered in San Juan Puerto Rico.
The Department of Economic Development and Commerce is headquartered in San Juan Puerto Rico.
The Public Buildings Authority is headquartered in San Juan Puerto Rico.

Under Act 2-2017 AAFAF acts as fiscal agent and financial advisor to the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico.
Under Act 2-2017 AAFAF acts as fiscal agent and financial advisor to PREPA as an instrumentality.
Under Act 2-2017 AAFAF acts as fiscal agent and financial advisor to PRASA as an instrumentality.
Under Act 2-2017 AAFAF acts as fiscal agent and financial advisor to the Highways and Transportation Authority as an instrumentality.
Under Act 2-2017 AAFAF acts as fiscal agent and financial advisor to COFINA as an instrumentality.
Under a public-private partnership operation and maintenance agreement LUMA Energy operates the PREPA electricity transmission and distribution system.
Under a public-private partnership agreement Genera PR operates the legacy PREPA power-generation fleet.
Autopistas Metropolitanas de Puerto Rico holds a long-term toll-road concession over the PR-22 and PR-5 highways under an agreement with the Highways and Transportation Authority.
The PREPA electricity grid reconstruction program addresses post-hurricane recovery of the transmission and distribution system.
The PRASA capital improvement program funds water and sewer infrastructure across Puerto Rico.
LUMA Energy operates and controls the PREPA transmission and distribution operation under its public-private partnership agreement.
Genera PR operates and controls the legacy PREPA power-generation operation under its public-private partnership agreement.
Autopistas Metropolitanas operates and controls the PR-22 and PR-5 toll-road concession under its agreement with the Highways and Transportation Authority.
